mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly boosting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 4.2% within a single month, it signals a rapid deterioration in labor market conditions that triggers recession fears, causing investors to sell equities and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises above 4.2% in a single month
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in more than 60% of triggered instances despite the negative signal, or the signal fails to predict drawdowns exceeding 3% in the subsequent month.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous contraction in industrial production and a decline in real personal savings rates indicates a supply-demand mismatch that compresses corporate profit margins, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative forward returns in more than 50% of historical occurrences, or the interaction term in a multivariate regression yields a p-value greater than 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of rapid rate hikes, a sustained increase in housing starts acts as a leading indicator of resilient consumer demand, which offsets tightening headwinds and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5% for two consecutive months
failure_condition: The positive return prediction reverses to negative when FEDFUNDS exceeds 5.0%, or the signal's predictive power vanishes when controlling for CPIAUCSL momentum.

mechanism_narrative: A sequential quarterly expansion in real GDP growth above 2.0% annualized rate reflects robust aggregate demand, which supports corporate revenue growth and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in expansion periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the CPI index exceeds 255.0, it signals entrenched inflation that forces the Federal Reserve to maintain restrictive policy, compressing equity multiples and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in more than 60% of triggered instances despite the negative signal, or the signal fails to predict drawdowns exceeding 3% in the subsequent month.

mechanism_narrative: A rising unemployment rate combined with declining real personal savings rates indicates a structural shift in household balance sheets that reduces consumption-driven earnings, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative forward returns in more than 50% of historical occurrences, or the interaction term in a multivariate regression yields a p-value greater than 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: During supply chain normalization phases, a sustained rise in industrial production paired with stable housing starts signals improved capacity utilization, which boosts corporate margins and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises above 105.0 for three consecutive months
failure_condition: The positive return prediction reverses to negative when INDPRO falls below 105.0, or the signal's predictive power vanishes when controlling for FEDFUNDS momentum.
